zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth CARPENTER (11thggm)於1552年出生在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 勞倫斯 也尤利 煙雨 聖 瑪麗 抹大拉的 米爾克 史崔特。她於1573年9月6日與William Woodcocke I在英國英格蘭倫敦易阿林聖 馬也斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1631年4月18日在英國英格蘭享年79歲。

nameMeanings.name: CARPENTER
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): occupational name for a worker in wood, Norman French carpentier (from Late Latin carpentarius ‘cartwright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Zimmermann  , French   Charpentier  , Italian   Carpentieri  , or cognates and equivalents in various other languages.

variants.name: Zimmermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Hans, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Klaus, Gerd, Manfred, Wolfgang, Bernhard.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a carpenter, Middle High German zimberman (a compound of zimber, zim(m)er ‘timber, wood’ + man ‘man’), German Zimmermann.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth CARPENTER (11thggm) was born in 1552, in St Lawrence Jewry with St Mary Magdalene Milk Street, London, England, United Kingdom. She married William Woodcocke I on 6 September 1573, in St Mary's, Ealing, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 18 April 1631, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

영국 잉글랜드 런던 올잉 스트 마이'스
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth CARPENTER (11thggm)(은)는 1552년 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 로렌스 제리 위스 스트 메리 막달레나 미크 스트릿에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1573년 9월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 올잉 스트 마이'스에서 William Woodcocke I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1631년 4월 18일 영국 잉글랜드에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

ст мэри'с, эалинг, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth CARPENTER (11thggm) родилась в 1552 г. в местности ст ларенс джюри вит ст мэри магдеилин милк стрит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Woodcocke I 6 сентября 1573 г. в местности ст мэри'с, эалинг, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 18 апреля 1631 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет.
